seoTitle: How to Add a Guest to a Trello Board
metaDescription: Invite clients or freelance partners to a single Trello board without giving them access to your whole workspace, using guest permissions.
intro: Working with a freelancer or client often means giving them access to one specific project without opening up your entire workspace. This guide covers inviting a single-board guest in Trello, so outside collaborators only see the board you choose.

steps.heading: Open your target board
steps.body: Navigate to the specific board you want to share with an external collaborator.

steps.heading: Click Share or Invite
steps.body: Find the Share or Invite button in the top header of the board.

steps.heading: Enter their email or username
steps.body: Type the exact email address or Trello username of the person you want to invite.

steps.heading: Confirm the access level
steps.body: Check the permission dropdown to make sure the invite stays locked to board-level access rather than the full workspace.

steps.heading: Send the invitation
steps.body: Add an optional note explaining the project scope, then click Send Invitation.

steps.heading: Verify their guest status
steps.body: Check the Board Members avatar bar for a new user row with a Guest badge, and confirm their single-board access in Workspace Members settings.

faq.question: Will a guest see my other private boards?
faq.answer: No, a single-board guest is limited to the exact board they were invited to and cannot see your other boards or workspace.

faq.question: How can I tell if someone is a guest versus a full member?
faq.answer: Guests appear in the Board Members list with a distinct Guest badge tag next to their name.

faq.question: Can I remove a guest's access later?
faq.answer: Yes, click their avatar on the board and choose the option to change permissions or remove them from the board.

faq.question: Can I invite someone using a shareable link instead of email?
faq.answer: Yes, Trello also supports shareable invitation links for quickly onboarding collaborative guest users.
